headlines.title: Venezuelans the US deported hours before earthquakes still missing
headlines.snippet: The US deported at least 146 people to Venezuela, just hours before two deadly earthquakes struck last Wednesday.

headlines.title: “WhatsApp Privacy Changes”
headlines.snippet: WhatsApp is rolling out usernames to keep phone numbers private.

headlines.title: Why India’s viral youth party is still protesting
headlines.snippet: India's Cockroach Janta Party protested for nearly two weeks, demanding the education minister's resignation. The party's specific demands are not detailed.

headlines.title: What is birthright citizenship, and what does the Supreme Court ruling say?
headlines.snippet: The nation's highest court has invalidated an executive order that sought to restrict birthright citizenship. This right is established in the US Constitution.

headlines.title: Emergency crews search rubble after Athens building collapse
headlines.snippet: Emergency crews are searching through rubble in Athens, Greece, following the collapse of a four-story apartment building. The search is for survivors.

headlines.title: Syria's president condemns Israeli attacks near Golan Heights
headlines.snippet: Syria's president condemns Israeli attacks near Golan Heights Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa says Syria "is neither a testing ground for foreign conspiracies nor a venue for carrying out the ambitions of others", according to Al Mayadeen.

headlines.title: US and Gulf states sanction entities accused of financial links to Hezbollah
headlines.snippet: US and Gulf states sanction entities accused of financial links to Hezbollah The US Department of the Treasury has announced joint sanctions by the Terrorist Financing Targeting Center against five entities and 16 individuals it claims are linked to Hezbollah's financial network.

headlines.title: Israeli drone attack kills two north of Khan Younis
headlines.snippet: An Israeli drone strike north of Khan Younis in southern Gaza has resulted in two deaths and several injuries, according to the Wafa news agency.

headlines.title: Iran says final talks depend on implementation of US-Iran MoU clauses
headlines.snippet: Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated that final talks are contingent on the implementation of specific clauses within a memorandum of understanding with the United States. These clauses pertain to ceasing hostilities and maritime passage.

headlines.title: US Supreme Court rejects Trump's effort to limit birthright citizenship
headlines.snippet: The US Supreme Court has rejected an effort by Trump to limit birthright citizenship. This decision addresses an executive order issued on his first day in office as part of an immigration crackdown.

headlines.title: Vance Says ‘More Game to Play’ in Iran Negotiations
headlines.snippet: Vance indicated that there is 'more game to play' in the ongoing negotiations with Iran.

headlines.snippet: Netanyahu stated that Israel will remain in southern Lebanon as long as Hezbollah poses a threat.

headlines.title: Syrian parliament set to announce Sharaa's pick of 70 MPs
headlines.snippet: The Syrian parliament is preparing to announce the selection of 70 Members of Parliament. This new legislature will then commence the process of debating and approving legislation.

headlines.title: Man arrested after arson attack on Dublin Islamic centre
headlines.snippet: A man has been arrested in connection with an arson attack on the Al Madinah Hall, an Islamic center in Dublin. The incident occurred after evening prayers, and no injuries were reported.

headlines.title: US Supreme Court rejects Trump bid to end birthright citizenship
headlines.snippet: The US Supreme Court has rejected Trump's attempt to end birthright citizenship. Chief Justice John Roberts emphasized that children born in the US are citizens at birth under the Fourteenth Amendment.

headlines.title: Iran prepares for late supreme leader's funeral later this week
headlines.snippet: Iran is making preparations for the funeral of its late supreme leader, Ayatollah Khamenei, which is scheduled to take place later this week. Representatives from approximately 30 countries are expected to attend.

headlines.title: Fresh Israeli strikes on Gaza set 200 tents ablaze despite truce
headlines.snippet: Israeli strikes in Gaza have set fire to over 200 tents, causing thousands to become homeless, despite a reported ceasefire.
